description: Mood taxonomy used by rag_categories.py for few-shot retrieval and by agent.py for classification. Example snippets are original handwritten text chosen to evoke each mood without reproducing copyrighted lyrics.

moods.name: Hype
moods.description: High-energy, adrenaline-forward tracks that push the listener to move, shout, or go harder.
examples:
Put your hands up, we don't stop 'til the sun comes back
Engine running hot, foot on the pedal, no looking back
Lights up in my eyes, I was born for the flash

Run it up, run it up, keep the bass on the floor
Every bone in my body says go go go
Tonight's the kind of night we've been waiting for

Pulse is pounding, heart's a hammer, I can't slow down
Stadium roaring, feet off the ground
We ride the wave 'til the city shuts down

Crank it up, let the neighbors hear it, shake the walls
No brakes, no maps, we just answer the call
Full speed, full send, we don't fall

moods.name: Chill
moods.description: Laid-back, low-tempo grooves that feel warm, spacious, and easy to live inside.
examples:
Sunday morning, coffee steaming, nowhere to be
Light leaks through the curtains slow, the world's on mute
Nothing's heavy, nothing's loud, just us and the tune

Window down, one hand out, the highway hums along
Clouds drift west, we drift with 'em, ain't a care in the song
Time moves gentle on a day this long

Couch of yours, record spinning soft and warm
Her laugh falls easy, mine falls after hers
Evening leans in slow, no rush, no storm

Golden hour on the rooftop, wind plays with her hair
City breathing quiet below, we don't go anywhere
Just a slow groove carrying nothing but the air

moods.name: Sad
moods.description: Melancholy, aching, introspective tracks centered on loss, longing, or loneliness.
examples:
Your side of the bed still holds the shape of you
I reach out at night and find the room too wide
Morning comes and the silence won't move

I kept every letter, I kept every song
But I couldn't keep you, and I don't know what I did wrong
The rain won't stop, and neither will I

Empty glass, empty hours, empty half of a bed
I play our old record so I can hear you again
Even ghosts stop visiting eventually

I thought love was supposed to last longer than this
Now I drive past your street and pretend I forgot where you live
Everything reminds me, everything hurts

moods.name: Angry
moods.description: Aggressive, confrontational, defiant tracks driven by fury, betrayal, or pushback.
examples:
I bit my tongue 'til it bled for you, now I'm done pretending
You lit the match, don't act surprised at where this is ending
Everything you touched, I'm burning

You said my name like it was a weapon, now watch me use mine
You built a throne out of everyone you broke to climb
The whole thing's coming down tonight

I'm not the kid you walked on, not the voice you silenced
I'm the answer you never wanted, loud and violent
Every wall you built is coming down

You can keep your sorry, keep your soft apology
I don't want peace, I want you to remember me
Every scar has a name and it's yours

moods.name: Romantic
moods.description: Warm, devoted, intimate tracks about love, closeness, and wanting someone specifically.
examples:
Every song I've ever loved was just rehearsal for you
I didn't know a heart could feel this full and this true
Stay, just stay, the rest will figure itself out

The way you laugh in the morning could rebuild a man
I'll spend the rest of my life just learning who you are
Hold my hand like you mean it — you do, I know you do

I'd choose you in any life, in any year, in any room
You're the only reason I trust anything good can be true
Come a little closer, let the world fall away

Slow dance in the kitchen, no music but the rain
Your head against my chest, and I forget my own name
Home isn't a place, it's the way you say mine

moods.name: Reflective
moods.description: Quiet, introspective tracks centered on thinking through something — wistful but not strictly sad.
examples:
I've been turning that night over in my head
The way the streetlight made the rain look slow
Some things you don't fix, you just let them sit

Walked the same block I walked at twenty-three
Different shoes, same questions, no answers yet
Time isn't a teacher, it's just a long road

Older now, but the kid is still in here
He asks me sometimes what we've been doing
I never quite know how to answer him

Sat in the parked car after the engine cooled
Thinking about choices like they were furniture
What I keep, what I leave by the curb

moods.name: Hopeful
moods.description: Uplifting, forward-looking tracks about possibility, healing, or starting over — without going full hype.
examples:
Tomorrow is a door we haven't opened yet
I packed light, I packed brave, I'm gonna find out
The morning is on its way and so are we

I survived the worst of me and there's still a song
Sun's coming up like it owes me something
Watch me, watch me, watch me run

Rebuild day by day, brick by brick, breath by breath
The window I broke is the window I'll glaze
I'm not who I was, and that's the whole point

Even the long winter eventually thaws
Even the loudest doubt eventually quiets down
Hold on, hold on, the green is coming back

moods.name: Playful
moods.description: Light, bouncy, quirky tracks that feel like a wink — fun without trying to be hype.
examples:
Skipping rocks across the morning, what's the rush
Wore my socks inside out just for the joke of it
Life's a little goofy, and so are we

Kazoo solo at the wedding, who said no
Snuck a dance move into the staff meeting
Why so serious, the world's a cartoon

Made up a song about my refrigerator
It's mostly about leftovers, but it has a hook
Don't take yourself too seriously, take the bridge

Polka-dot socks, two scoops, second breakfast
Found a quarter, called it a sign from the universe
Tuesday is a costume party if you let it
